machinery_genes.role: tail assembly chaperone / tube-sheath cap; membrane-complex-interacting
machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Re-anchored to the founding paper (Zheng & Leung 2007), which names EvpA directly. TssA family label is from the Santos 2024 Evp->Tss mapping (corroborating, in papers[]).

machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: EvpB is one of the 16 contiguous evp-cluster genes / 13 secretion-essential apparatus proteins defined in Zheng & Leung 2007 (not individually named in the abstract). TssB/VipA sheath family label from the Santos 2024 mapping. Lower-confidence: family from review, existence-as-apparatus from the founding paper.

machinery_genes.quote: the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: Zheng & Leung 2007 founding paper explicitly names EvpC as the Hcp homologue. Independently confirmed by the EvpC structure paper (Jobichen et al 2010, PMID 20886112: 'EvpC ... shares high sequence similarity with Hcp1'). Hcp is secreted tube and counts as machinery per the T6SS brief, NOT as an effector.

machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: One of the 16 evp-cluster genes / 13 secretion-essential apparatus proteins (Zheng & Leung 2007), not individually named in the abstract. TssC/VipB sheath family label from the Santos 2024 mapping.

machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: One of the 16 evp-cluster genes / 13 secretion-essential apparatus proteins (Zheng & Leung 2007), not individually named in the abstract. TssE baseplate family label from the Santos 2024 mapping.

machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: One of the 16 evp-cluster genes (Zheng & Leung 2007). ClpV (TssH) identity from the Santos 2024 review ('EvpH is a homolog of ClpV'). ClpV counted as machinery per the brief.

machinery_genes.quote: two additional secreted proteins (EvpI, a homologue of VgrG, and EvpP) were found
machinery_genes.note: Zheng & Leung 2007 founding paper names EvpI as the VgrG homologue. VgrG spike is secreted machinery (tube tip), counted per the T6SS brief; not an effector.

machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Re-anchored to the founding paper (Zheng & Leung 2007), which names EvpL directly as an EvpO-interacting membrane-complex partner. TssJ family label from the Santos 2024 mapping.

machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins including EvpC (a homologue of Hcp) and EvpI but not EvpD and EvpJ
machinery_genes.note: One of the 16 evp-cluster genes / 13 secretion-essential apparatus proteins (Zheng & Leung 2007), not individually named in the abstract. TssK baseplate family label from the Santos 2024 mapping.

machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Re-anchored to the founding paper (Zheng & Leung 2007), which names EvpN directly as an EvpO-interacting membrane-complex partner. TssL/DotU family label from the Santos 2024 mapping.

machinery_genes.quote: EvpO, a putative ATPase which contained a Walker A motif, showed possible interactions with three EVP proteins (EvpA, EvpL and EvpN)
machinery_genes.note: Re-anchored to the founding paper (Zheng & Leung 2007), which names EvpO directly and gives its ATPase role. TssM/IcmF family label from the Santos 2024 mapping (EvpO ~ VasK/IcmF1).

machinery_genes.role: non-secreted apparatus component (no Tss homolog named)
machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins
machinery_genes.note: EvpF is one of the 13 EVP proteins required for T6SS secretion (only EvpD and EvpJ are dispensable; EvpP is the effector). Santos 2024 Table 1 lists EvpF as 'N/A' for a unified-Tss homolog, so no specific component family is assigned. Lower-confidence call: named as an essential EVP apparatus gene but without a Tss family. Included as core apparatus, family left as unassigned.

machinery_genes.role: non-secreted apparatus component (no Tss homolog named)
machinery_genes.quote: We systematically mutagenized all the 16 EVP genes and found that the secretion of EvpP was dependent on 13 EVP proteins
machinery_genes.note: EvpK is among the 13 EVP proteins essential for secretion (Santos 2024: 'EvpA-EvpC, EvpE-EvpI, and EvpK-EvpO, are essential for the secretion of EvpC, EvpI, and EvpP'). Santos Table 1 marks EvpK 'N/A' for a unified-Tss homolog, so family unassigned. Lower-confidence call, same rationale as EvpF.

notes: System: the EVP (Edwardsiella virulence protein) gene cluster is the single T6SS of Edwardsiella piscicida EIB202 (=E. tarda EIB202; the species was reclassified after the original work). The founding/characterisation paper is Zheng & Leung 2007 (Mol Microbiol, PMID 17986187), which defined the 16-ORF EVP cluster (evpA-evpP) as a T6SS in E. tarda strain PPD130/91 and named the three secreted proteins EvpC (Hcp homolog), EvpI (VgrG homolog) and EvpP. EIB202 carries the orthologous EVP locus; the curated effectors for this instance (ETAE_0972,0975,0976,0977) sit in/around this locus. The strain-level apparatus-to-Tss mapping used for the unassigned-by-Zheng genes is Santos 2024 (Philippine J Fisheries) Table 1, which gives an explicit Evp->unified-Tss correspondence for 'Edwardsiella spp.' in the section headed 'Edwardsiella piscicida (old name E. tarda)'. The Santos review is not indexed in PubMed (no PMID) but its DOI 10.31398/tpjf/31.2.2024A0008 resolves. Core apparatus covered gene-by-gene: tube EvpC/Hcp, spike EvpI/VgrG, sheath EvpB/TssB + EvpE/TssC, baseplate EvpG/TssE + EvpM/TssK, membrane complex EvpL/TssJ + EvpN/TssL + EvpO/TssM, ATPase EvpH/ClpV, cap/chaperone EvpA/TssA. EvpF and EvpK are listed as essential-for-secretion EVP apparatus genes but Santos Table 1 assigns them no unified-Tss homolog ('N/A'), so they are included with family left unassigned (lowest-confidence machinery calls). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: EvpP (the secreted T6SS effector targeting macrophage inflammasome; matches this instance's effector context, not machinery); EvpD and EvpJ (Zheng & Leung 2007 show both are NOT required for T6SS-dependent secretion, i.e. dispensable, and Santos lists them 'N/A' for a Tss homolog); delivered toxin effectors EseL/EseM and EvpQ (Tse/effector-class, encoded on a separate genomic island); immunity proteins. No PAAR-repeat tip protein is named in the E. tarda/piscicida EVP literature, so PAAR is not listed. QA RE-ANCHORING (main-session integrity pass): the original subagent draft cited 9 of the 13 genes to the Santos 2024 review (DOI 10.31398/tpjf/31.2.2024A0008), which is not PubMed-indexed and whose 'quotes' were reformatted Table-1 rows. All 13 genes are now primary-cited to the founding paper Zheng & Leung 2007 (PMID 17986187): EvpC/EvpI (secreted tube/spike) and EvpA/EvpL/EvpN/EvpO (named in the EvpO Walker-A ATPase interaction sentence) carry individual naming quotes; EvpB/EvpE/EvpG/EvpH/EvpK/EvpM/EvpF carry the paper's collective '16 EVP genes / 13 secretion-essential apparatus proteins' quote, with their Evp->Tss family labels supplied by the Santos 2024 mapping (retained in papers[] as a corroborating review). Status downgraded COMPLETE->PARTIAL to reflect that 7 family assignments rest on the founding paper's collective statement plus a review mapping rather than individual primary-paper naming quotes (abstract-only access; the per-gene table is in the Zheng 2007 full text, which was not retrievable). The contiguous EVP locus is fully located for Phase 1. Curation is literature-only, independent of MacSyFinder/Pfam scans and without mining the RefSeq genome.
